channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Legacy news on its heels as second Trump era begins, but not abandoning mission
item.description: It's a rough time for legacy news outlets as Donald Trump prepares to take the presidential oath for a second term.
item.dc:creator: David Bauder, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Trump returns to Washington for celebrations ahead of inauguration
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Donald Trump returned to Washington Saturday evening to celebrate with family, supporters and political allies ahead of his second inauguration, a triumphant return for the Republican four years after he departed the city under the shadow of an attack by his supporters on the Capit
item.dc:creator: MICHELLE L. PRICE and WILL WEISSERT, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Marco Rubio met with UAP whistleblower prior to Trump nomination
item.description: Barber said he’s worked in both an official and unofficial capacity for the U.S. government and partners in the intelligence community.
item.dc:creator: Ross Coulthart, NewsNation

item.title: Thousands of left-wing demonstrators descend on Washington to protest Trump inauguration
item.description: Thousands protesters advocating for a range of left wing causes have descended on Washington, D.C., to protest President–elect Trump’s inauguration on Monday.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Judges allow some Capitol riot defendants to return to DC for Trump's inauguration
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Thousands of Donald Trump supporters stormed the U.S. Capitol after he lost the 2020 presidential election. Four years later, some of them are allowed to return to the nation's capital so they can celebrate Trump's return to the White House.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL KUNZELMAN, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: State lawmaker has House chamber ban revoked after viral scuffle left him hospitalized
item.description: A Georgia state senator has had his ban from entering the state House chamber revoked following an ugly incident on Thursday which saw him flung to the floor, arrested and subsequently hospitalized.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: With a US ban on TikTok hours away, Trump says he 'most likely' will grant an extension
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump says he “most likely” will give TikTok 90 more days to work out a deal that would allow the popular video-sharing platform to avoid a U.S. ban.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Porn case in the Supreme Court this week is about protecting children, says Republican AG
item.description: Texas Attorney General Ken Paxton is fighting to uphold a Texas law he says is keeping the pornography industry from targeting children with harmful content.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump's family circle has a different look as he returns to the White House
item.description: As Trump prepares to return to the White House, his family circle looks a little different than it did eight years ago.
item.dc:creator: Darlene Superville And Adriana Gomez Licon, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Merrick Garland exits with his record under scrutiny and the Justice Department bracing for upheaval
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — During hearings on Merrick Garland's nomination to be President Joe Biden's attorney general, the longtime federal appeals court judge told senators in 2021 that he hoped to “turn down the volume” on the public discourse about the Justice Department and return to the days when the
item.dc:creator: ALANNA DURKIN RICHER, ERIC TUCKER and COLLEEN LONG, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Ohio Lt. Gov. Jon Husted will succeed JD Vance in the US Senate
item.description: COLUMBUS, Ohio (AP) — Republican Ohio Lt. Gov. Jon Husted will succeed Vice President-elect JD Vance in the U.S. Senate, the state's governor announced Friday. Gov.
item.dc:creator: JULIE CARR SMYTH and STEPHEN GROVES, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Minnesota Sen. Nicole Mitchell trial delayed until after legislative session
item.description: A judge has granted DFL Senator Nicole Mitchell’s request to delay her trial until after the Minnesota legislative session ends.
item.dc:creator: Clay Masters, MPR News

item.title: Sam Altman's OpenAI backing initiative headed by several anti-Trump staff pushing liberal causes
item.description: Sam Altman's OpenAI is backing a new AI initiative from a group where John Kerry was a founding member that has voiced support for liberal causes in the past.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Commerce Secretary Raimondo offers a parting shot at Trump and advice for Democrats' future
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — When Commerce Secretary Gina Raimondo looks at Donald Trump, she says she sees a president-elect who for all of his aggressive talk is afraid to let America compete with the rest of the world, responding instead with tariffs and curtailing immigration.
item.dc:creator: JOSH BOAK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: The Senate advances a migrant detention bill that could be Trump's first law to sign
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Senate is heading toward a final vote on legislation that would give states the power to challenge federal immigration policies and require federal authorities to detain migrants accused of theft and violent crimes, setting a new tone on immigration as Donald Trump enters the W
item.dc:creator: STEPHEN GROVES, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Live updates: Noem pledges to eliminate CBP One app; Senate advances Laken Riley Act
item.description: South Dakota Gov. Kristi Noem (R), President-elect Trump's pick to head the Department of Homeland Security (DHS), is appearing for her confirmation hearing before a Senate panel Friday. The purview of DHS involves immigration and federal emergency management — two hot-button topics.
item.dc:creator: The Hill staff, NewsNation

item.title: List: Tech moguls attending Trump's inauguration
item.description: The heads of five of the top eight largest companies (by market cap) reportedly plan to attend his inauguration on Jan. 20.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Soriano, NewsNation

item.title: Higher stakes stoke passions at DNC forum
item.description: With just over 2 weeks until the DNC chooses new officers, candidates for chair know stakes have been raised.
item.dc:creator: Jackie Koppell, NewsNation

item.title: Biden sets record by commuting sentences of nearly 2,500 people convicted on nonviolent drug charges
item.description: President Joe Biden is commuting the sentences of almost 2,500 people convicted of nonviolent drug offenses. Friday’s clemency actions give Biden the presidential record for most individual pardons and commutations issued.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Biden commutes the sentences of nearly 2,500 non-violent drug offenders
item.description: Biden said he is proud of his record on clemency and will “continue to review additional commutations and pardons” before he leaves office on Monday.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Biden won't enforce TikTok ban, official says, leaving fate of app to Trump
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Joe Biden won't enforce a ban on the social media app TikTok that is set to take effect a day before he leaves office on Monday, a U.S. official said Thursday, leaving its fate in the hands of President-elect Donald Trump.
item.dc:creator: ZEKE MILLER, JOSH BOAK, MICHELLE L. PRICE and MARY CLARE JALONICK, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Moody announces Senate plans, DeSantis names AG replacement
item.description: After weeks of speculation, Gov. Ron DeSantis appointed Ashley Moody to replace Marco Rubio, who plans to leave his senate seat.
item.dc:creator: Ty Russell, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Court aims for fall trial date in sexual misconduct lawsuit against Fletcher
item.description: The lawsuit facing Nathan Fletcher over allegations of sexual misconduct could move to a civil trial as soon as fall 2025, as both sides continue to bump heads over text messages between the ex-supervisor's accuser and a friend.
item.dc:creator: Danielle Dawson, Fox 5

item.title: Trump taps Voight, Gibson and Stallone as 'special envoys' to Hollywood
item.description: "These three very talented people will be my eyes and ears, and I will get done what they suggest. It will again be, like The United States of America itself, The Golden Age of Hollywood!" Trump posted.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, NewsNation

item.title: Trump’s Inauguration Gala: The hottest ticket in town and the biggest billionaires ball ever
item.description: And his new friends are putting their money where their mouths are — the big tech boys have donated millions to Trump’s inauguration fund.
item.dc:creator: Paula Froelich, NewsNation

item.title: Gov. Murphy calling for cellphone ban in NJ classrooms
item.description: NEWARK, N.J. (PIX11) - Could there be a day when there will be no cellphones in New Jersey classrooms? In his State of the State address on Tuesday, Gov. Phil Murphy announced his support for legislation that would direct school districts to adopt policies that ban cellphones in K-12 classrooms.
item.dc:creator: Jim Vasil, PIX 11

item.title: NYC mayor: More beds for mentally ill people, more cops on subways
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) -- With more NYPD officers heading into the subway system during the overnight hours, New York City Mayor Eric Adams said he is also looking to get mentally ill homeless people off the trains with a new option.
item.dc:creator: Henry Rosoff, PIX 11

item.title: Group behind failed Florida recreational marijuana amendment trying again for 2026
item.description: The group behind the 2024 ballot measure that would have enshrined recreational marijuana access in the Florida Constitution is giving it another shot in 2026.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Tucker, WFLA, NBC

item.title: DFLer Doron Clark, Republican Abigail Wolters win primary in Senate District 60 special election
item.description: A special primary election Tuesday narrowed the field of candidates running for a Minnesota Senate seat from nine to two.
item.dc:creator: MPR News Staff, MPR News

item.title: Trump will begin his presidency in delicate position, poll finds
item.description: A new NPR/PBS News/Marist poll shows that Americans' support for President-elect Donald Trump's top priorities is split, despite his claims of a mandate for his agenda.
item.dc:creator: Domenico Montanaro, MPR News

item.title: Gov. Murphy lays out ambitious goals in State of the State address
item.description: TRENTON, N.J. (PIX11) - Gov. Phil Murphy on Tuesday delivered his State of the State address for the last time with a full year at the helm ahead of him.
item.dc:creator: Jim Vasil, PIX 11

item.title: NY plan would make community college free: Who qualifies
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – Gov. Kathy Hochul on Tuesday announced a plan to make community college free for adults pursuing in-demand careers.
item.dc:creator: Finn Hoogensen, PIX 11

item.title: Trump claims California had the chance to 'maintain their forests' amid Los Angeles wildfires
item.description: “The problem is it's so dry, it was always so dry there, and it's just … it's just a mess. They could’ve, you know, maintained their forests, and — as I said they have to do,” Trump said.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, Fox 5

item.title: Judge: DFL candidate’s election stands for Shakopee-area Minnesota House seat
item.description: State Rep. Brad Tabke, a DFL Minnesota House incumbent who narrowly won reelection, should be able to begin a new term, a judge ruled Tuesday — despite a GOP election challenge centering on a pool of ballots that were cast but never counted.
item.dc:creator: Dana Ferguson and MPR News Staff, MPR News

item.title: What to make of the final polling of the Biden years
item.description: A January 10-11 survey of 1,000 registered voters found President Joe Biden with a 37% approval rating in the final week of his presidency, 12 points lower than when Emerson first polled his approval in 2021.
item.dc:creator: Bill Disbrow, Fox 5

item.title: Judge clears the way for release of special counsel Smith's report on Trump's Jan. 6 case
item.description: The Justice Department can publicly release its investigative report on President-elect Donald Trump's 2020 election interference case, a federal judge said Monday — the latest ruling in a court dispute over the highly anticipated document days before Trump is set to take office again.
item.dc:creator: ALANNA DURKIN RICHER and ERIC TUCKER Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: DeSantis's call for special session 'premature,' Senate President, House Speaker say
item.description: Florida Gov. Ron DeSantis announced a special legislative session to address illegal immigration, among other issues, on Monday.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Tucker, WFLA, NBC
